title: Level 680
wikitext:
[[Category:Class 3e - Environmental]][[Category:Dangerous]][[Category:Frigid]][[Category:Winter]][[Category:Environmental Hazards]][[Category:Phenomena]][[Category:Objects]][[Category:Darkness]][[Category:Levels]][[Category:Cluster I]][[Category:Mysterious]]
{{User:JakitoFan/Class Custom/4|class=3e - Environmental|hexagon=3e|header=Threat Index: Class|safety=Unsafe|security=Unsecure|entity=Environmental Threats|bg-image=159hall.png|bg-size=1738|text-color=#1e2222|chevron=❄}} {{Blurimage|file=Level 446.jpg|caption=An image of Bezdna.}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Bezdna}}__NOTOC____NOEDITSECTION__ Level 680, known colloquially as 'Bezdna', is the 681st level of the Backrooms, and presumably an extension of [[Level 445]]. ==Description== Level 680 consists of an expansive city centered around a singular road presumably from the state of Russia within [[The Frontrooms]]. The city is covered in an eternal, thick layer of snow, and a constant mild snowstorm is prevalent throughout the level; some areas are snowed in more than others, although it is impossible to find a clear area. The average temperature of the level is -8°F, with recorded lows going as cold as -38°F. The snowed-in town in permeated by a complex of roads and bridges of concrete, with some having collapsed into the snow. While technically traversable, it is unadvised, as serious slipping hazards arise from walking on the iced and snowed-over bridges. Level 680's geometry can be described as cylindrical; if one walks down the central road within the level, they will continue onwards infinitely. Meanwhile, attempting to far enough away from the highway will result in one returning back to it. The level has been measured to be around 1.8 miles in width, and is theoretically infinite in length. ==Structures & Objects==
  • Khrushchyovkas
  • Snegozaderzhateli
  • Aurora Drehnahlis
{{Blurimage|file=159hall.png|caption=An image of a hallway within a Khrushchyovka.|align=left|width=300px}} The major environmental feature of the level is the Khrushchyovkas, large apartment buildings made of brick and consistent with their [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] counterparts. The Khrushchyovkas consist of a lobby, tight hallways, more open stairwells and dozens of cramped and uncomfortable apartments. The apartments of Level 680 are small, usually only containing a small bedroom, bathroom, and kitchen, although often in a state of disarray or destruction. The contents of the apartments are scarce; the most common objects appear to be small crackers and cookies within kitchens, and small cubes of soap within the bathrooms. These apartments are technically livable, owing to the fact that contents of the apartments have been recorded to be self-replenishing, although they regenerate far too slow to sustain wanderers for extended durations of time. For one to fully take up residence, they would have to forage often and thoroughly.
{{Blurimage|file=Стоп.png|caption=An image of a Snowstop.|align=left|width=300px}}
A separate environmental aspect of the level is the Snowstops. These are small to medium sized bus stops, placed nowhere in particular; it is not an uncommon sight to see one facing the wall of a building or away from the nearest roads. These stops usually have a place to sit in the form of a bench, a small stock of newspapers from various periods of time, and an illegible bus stop schedule. These fixtures are written in a yet-unknown language known as Bezdniazik, or 'Abyss-speak', appearing to adopt the Cyrillic alphabet. No other forms of language within the level are written in this language, making deciphering it almost impossible. These Snowstops are always covered in a thin layer of ice, and snow tends to crowd around them. The Snowstops rarely have discarded suitcases or lunch boxes containing foods, or a non-functional rotary phone.
{{Blurimage|file=159AB.png|caption=An image of Aurora Drehnahlis hovering over a set of Khrushchyovkas, with a lot of abandoned cars in tow.|align=left|width=300px}} Aurora Drehnahlis is a rare phenomenon that occurs within the skies of the level. During this phenomenon, a set of glowing lights similar to that of the Aurora Borealis phenomenon from the Frontrooms appears to illuminate the light sky, dancing in waving patterns slowly yet surely. The Aurora Drehnahlis is primarily a shade of blue-green, with red-tinted outlines. While the phenomenon is active, the level goes under a significant change. Snowfall within the level increases rapidly as the temperature plummets, and ice tends to grow during this period of time. Fiolgine gas temporarily enters a dormant state, posing no threats to wanderers as it withdraws into broken pipes. This phenomenon lasts ~3 hours, after which the level will return to normal.
===Gigakhrushchevka=== {{Blurquote|image=Bezdna2.jpg|quote-text="The Gigakhrushchevka doesn't stop growing."|person=???|size=1000px}} The Gigakhrushchevka is a gargantual variation of the Khrushchyovkas that permeate the level, located at the western edge of the level. The Gigakhrushchevka has a single front entrance at the center, being a rusted blue door with steps leading up to it and a perpetually-glowing window beside it. The interior of the Gigakhrushchevka is far more spacious, the apartments, hallways, lobby and stairwell have grown in size. This is most likely because with the vastly larger size of the Gigakhrushchevka, the building has more room for hallways or apartments to work with. The primary, anomalous feature of the Gigakhrushchevka is that it expands constantly, in all directions but east. The exterior walls constantly expand at an estimated rate of ~0.012 miles per year. Since the level's discovery on the 9th of November 2025, the Gigakhrushchevka has expanded by ~32 miles. Not only does the Gigakhrushchevka contain most of the exits to the level, but it is also the common place for finding Fiolgine gas. Notably, certain sections of the Gigakhrushchevka are completely hollow and exhibit similar appearances to the exterior. The floor of these areas is nonexistent, with only a network of concrete walkways suspended above an inky black void preventing one from falling in. These areas are free from Fiolgine, but hazardous nonetheless. These areas are referred to as 'Interiors'. They tend to have more volatile geometry and unstable architecture, with incidents of walkways disintegrating under wanderers' feet or rubble crushing them from above not uncommon to hear.
==Entity Populations== Certain hypotheses state that the Gigakhrushchevka is itself an entity due to its ever-expanding nature, but this is disputed. Other than this, there are no entities present anywhere within the level, though for what reason is unknown. ==Colonies and Outposts== Currently, there are no colonies or outposts on Level 680.
===Nilbuilders=== *Former group within Level 680 *~15 members at its peak residing inside the Gigakhrushchevka *Dedicated to researching the level, the Gigakhrushchevka in particular *Failed due to their members falling into the inky black void of the Interior. ===M.E.G. Base Minus-Five-on-Ten=== *Former group within Level 680 *~12 members at its peak living a nomadic lifestyle *Dedicated to researching the level as a whole *Failed due to their members suffocating on the Fiolgine gas ===Nomadic Groups=== *A broad term for the myriad of nomads that have tried to populate Level 680 *~36 recorded attempts, all failed *Attempted to live in Level 680 *Consistently failed due to the level's hazards ==Entrances and Exits== The vast majority of exits appear within the bounds of the Gigakhrushchevka, while entrances have more freedom, depositing one in a random position in the level. The full list is featured below.
===Entrances=== Entrances into this level are hard to locate, but will surface with time. *In [[Level 445]], traveling down a dirt road lined with trees will seamlessly transition the level into Level 680. *In [[Level 245]], snowdrifts may randomly appear, noclipping into the snow will lead one to Level 680. *In [[Level 173]], often in close proximity to exits to [[Level 445]], patches of ice can be found floating. Interacting with them will cause a wanderer to black out and wake up on a bench in a Snegozaderzhateli. *In [[Level 690]], snow may occasionally spawn atop a bridge, jumping into it will lead here. *In [[Level 258]], staying inside a building at night may lead to the Gigakhrushchevka in Level 680. This transition is seamless and often goes unnoticed until the wanderer acknowledges the apparent size of the building, or when they leave the Gigakhrushchevka.
===Exits=== *Traveling deep inside the Gigakhrushchevka and finding a glitched mass of objects may lead to [[Level 404]], alternatively leading to [[The Broken]]. *[[Level 684]]'s entrances manifest as weathered paintings of, or advertisements for, image galleries or museums, hanging in the Gigakhrushchevka *Waiting too long at a Snegozaderzhateli will condemn you to waiting, [[Level 179|forever]]. *Reminiscing of [[The Frontrooms|better times]] while walking the Gigakhrushchevka may segue into [[Level 193]], [[Level 201]] or [[Level 231]]. *Rarely, finding a subway entrance along the roads may lead to [[Level 95]]. *Extremely rarely, garage doors attached to the sides of KhrushchyovkasIncluding the Gigakhrushchevka. will lead to the garages of [[Level 158]].
